column4: discipline-choice card ([vis]/[tha]) counts as the chosen discipline for all purposes once played; general but no section owns multi-discipline-choice card classification; nearest 1.3 (card types/multi-type cards)

card-conferred status "when played" (eg chosen for a contract) persists as a fact, vs the same status conferred by an ongoing in-play effect does not; watch-list candidate flagged in calibration (single instance) — nearest 1.6's play-vs-in-play requirement distinction, but this is about conferred status, not requirements

Orun/Aye clan-restricted cards cannot be placed on him while he is not Laibon; ordinary clan-requirement application, no transfer beyond Laibon-restricted cards on a non-Laibon-counting vampire; fails non-obviousness
specific capacity-threshold interaction with {Call the Great Beast}'s own burn/counter mechanic; fails transfer — affects no other card
card-specific interaction/reversal ruling for {No Secrets From the Magaji}; a one-off reversal, not a template — fails transfer
his cost-reduction ability restricted to masters played by his own controller; restates the ordinary controller-scoped reading of "his controller", fails non-obviousness

ability usable only when its own vampire is personally a combat participant, not merely when combat is occurring anywhere; recurs (White Lily.2) but no section owns ability-eligibility tied to self-combat-participation; nearest 4.1
same gap as Watenda.4 — ability restricted to use while its own bearer is personally in combat

his innate vote bonus and a title's vote bonus stack additively; cumulative/stacking effects is a watch-list gap (cf. Betrayer.3) — nothing owns effect-stacking generally; nearest 1.2 (periodicity/templates, by calibration convention) and 5.8 (title-granted votes)
her unlock trigger keyed to a specific action (defecting to become Anarch); card-specific reflex condition, fails transfer
a mandatory-bleed obligation ends once fulfilled, freeing normal action-taking; no section owns forced/mandatory-action obligations against normal turn structure; nearest 3.7.1 (bleed actions)
a different bleed-granting effect does not satisfy a distinct mandatory-bleed obligation; same gap as .1/.3
if the mandatory bleed cannot be performed (already bled this turn), she is locked out of acting entirely; same mandatory-action-obligation gap
being locked by one effect doesn't prevent normal unlock afterward; restates default lock/unlock behavior, fails non-obviousness
